name: Noor
context: junior backend developer, first job; threads: probation review, houseplant collection, driving test

episodes.text: Onboarding week done: laptop configured, repos cloned, and my first ticket merged, a one-line logging fix that I screenshotted like a diploma.

probes.query: What was my first merged ticket at the new job?
I feel bright hope thinking of that first merged change.
One tiny contribution with my name on it, saved like a trophy, resurfaces when I doubt myself.

episodes.text: Bought a monstera from the flower market and named her Clementine. Repotted her on newspaper on the kitchen floor, roots like pale noodles, dirt absolutely everywhere.

probes.query: What did I name the monstera from the flower market?
I feel cozy contentment recalling that repotting mess.
Soil under my nails and a new green housemate — that afternoon drifts back to me.

episodes.text: Deployed a migration that locked the users table for eleven minutes in production. Senior devs were kind, the postmortem was kind, and I still rewrote my apology four times.

probes.query: How long did my migration lock the users table?
I still burn with embarrassment remembering that production incident.
The damage I caused and couldn't undo keeps replaying every release I push out.

episodes.text: First driving lesson. Stalled twice at the same intersection, and the instructor said my mirror checks were textbook, which I am choosing to make my whole identity.

probes.query: What did the driving instructor call textbook?
I feel jittery but proud remembering that first lesson behind the wheel.
The engine dying twice at one crossing stays with me every time I buckle in.

episodes.text: Weekly chores plus a fridge purge of three science experiments formerly known as leftovers. Watered all nine plants and rotated Clementine a quarter turn toward the light.

probes.query: How many plants did I water on chore night?
I feel tidy calm thinking of that chore evening.
The small ceremony of turning each pot toward the window comes back to me.

episodes.text: Shipped the rate-limiter feature I owned end to end. Code review had one comment: nice. I walked home the long way and bought a pastry shaped like a fish.

probes.query: What shape was the pastry I bought after shipping the feature?
I feel glowing pride recalling the day my feature shipped.
Something I built alone running out there for everyone keeps straightening my back.

episodes.text: Clementine has root rot. Emergency surgery on the balcony at ten at night: trimmed the mush, repotted into dry mix, and lit a candle for her like a maniac.

probes.query: What did Clementine come down with?
I feel anxious fondness remembering that midnight plant surgery.
Kneeling over a sick green thing under a tiny flame drifts back whenever leaves droop.

episodes.text: Failed the driving test: reverse parallel park, clipped the cone at the very end of an otherwise clean run. The examiner said almost with genuine sympathy, and I rebooked for June.

probes.query: Which maneuver failed my driving test?
I still feel the sting of disappointment from that failed test.
One orange marker at the finish undid a whole smooth attempt, and it nags me in queues.

episodes.text: Team offsite: escape room where I found the magnet key everyone walked past, then barbecue where the CTO grilled and undercooked with total confidence.

probes.query: What did I find in the escape room that everyone missed?
I feel cheerful belonging remembering that team offsite.
Being the one who noticed what the rest overlooked comes back to me in meetings.

episodes.text: Prepped my probation review portfolio: twelve merged PRs, the incident postmortem with my remediation items all closed, and a one-pager on the rate limiter.

probes.query: How many merged PRs went into my probation portfolio?
I feel composed thinking of assembling that review folder.
Laying out months of my work on one desk to be judged floats back before evaluations.

episodes.text: Passed probation. My manager said the incident response impressed them more than the feature, handed me a permanent badge, and the team stuck a bow on my monitor.

probes.query: What did the team stick on my monitor after I passed probation?
I feel elated relief every time that probation result comes to mind.
The day my desk stopped being borrowed territory keeps glowing in me.

episodes.text: Clementine unfurled a new leaf, the first since the rot, with two proper holes in it. I photographed it from six angles like a proud relative at a graduation.

probes.query: How many holes did Clementine's new leaf have?
I feel disproportionate joy recalling that new leaf appearing.
Green proof that the patient pulled through keeps brightening my mornings.

episodes.text: Passed the driving retest, parallel park included, cone untouched. Drove Mom around the block exactly once with both hands at ten and two while she applauded.

probes.query: Who did I drive around the block after passing the retest?
I feel victorious remembering the retest and that lap with Mom.
The marker that stayed upright this time keeps replaying like a highlight reel.

episodes.text: Meal-prepped for the week, dusted every leaf in the apartment jungle, and set up autopay for the new car insurance. Extremely licensed behavior.

probes.query: What did I set up autopay for?
I feel settled calm thinking of that Sunday of small upgrades.
A quiet weekend of grown-up housekeeping drifts through my mind at month's start.
